changeLog: Controlled autonomous REAPER Lua execution, with safety hardening. New reaper_lua_execute tool: when the dedicated tools can't express a task, the assistant runs a short generated Lua snippet against REAPER's API. The environment is heavily restricted — no io/os/package/require/debug/coroutine, no pcall/load, no while/repeat/goto, no external-process APIs, no background defer loops, no JS_*/BR_Win32_*/SNM_* APIs, no action system (Main_OnCommand/MIDIEditor_OnCommand/NamedCommandLookup), no ReaPack_*, no script registration, no directory creation. Standard libraries are injected as copies so generated code can't poison globals. Each run is wrapped in one Undo block, captures print output (capped), caps reaper API calls, and the preflight rejects large numeric literals that would drive runaway loops. Every execution must be confirmed by the user before it runs (the generated code is shown in a native dialog; disable via ExtState MCAssistant/lua_exec_confirm=0). Security blocks are answered locally to avoid provider hangs. Note: a CPU loop with a computed bound can still freeze REAPER if approved — review the confirmation before running.
